name: go_vet
description: Run go vet on a Go project to check for common errors and suspicious constructs. Supports package paths (e.g. ./..., ./pkg/...), build tags, and output limiting to prevent token overflow.
when_to_use: Use after modifying Go source files to check for common issues before running tests. Replaces 'cd <project> && go vet ./... 2>&1 | head -30' with a single script call.

parameters.name: project_root
parameters.description: Root directory of the Go project (e.g. /app)

parameters.name: package_path
parameters.description: Package path to vet (default: './...'). Use '.' for root package only, or './pkg/...' for a subpackage.

parameters.name: --max
parameters.description: Maximum number of output lines to show (default: 30, use 0 for unlimited)

examples.expected: Vet all packages in /app, showing up to 30 lines of output.
Vet only packages under /app/pkg/, showing up to 30 lines.
Vet all packages with build tags kqueue and dev.
Vet only the root package, showing up to 50 lines.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-3 separate commands (cd to project, go vet with package path, optional head/tail for output filtering) with a single script call. The built-in --max flag prevents token overflow from verbose vet output, eliminating a separate head/grep step.
